old_claim.note: Experimental Controls provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The controls node is now scoped to Lind-style comparative trials in 1747 and therefore cannot enable the graph's early-modern pharmacology node dated around 1600.

source_shape.source_locator: The historical review identifies Lind's 1747 scurvy comparison as the controlled clinical trial milestone and describes its common conditions and six treatment arms.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors the corrected controlled-trial scope to 1747, well after the dependent node's 1600 chronology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later comparative-trial method cannot be a prerequisite for the earlier remedy-comparison practice; the latter retains casebook and iatrochemical context.

ontology_before: Experimental Pharmacology depended on an Experimental Controls node whose old placeholder date concealed the chronological reversal.
ontology_after: Early-modern pharmacology remains linked to period-appropriate medical records and iatrochemistry, while controlled comparative trials begin later.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: experimental_pharmacology no longer directly depends on experimental_controls.
would_reject_if: Experimental pharmacology is redated and rescoped to post-1747 controlled therapeutic trials.
A source establishes a controlled comparative design matching the corrected node scope before the dependent's 1600 anchor.
short_reason: The corrected controls milestone is 1747 and cannot enable a node anchored around 1600.
